new_beliefs.name: kind-type-table
new_beliefs.claim: Kind binds mood: each belief kind maps to the structural types it may inhabit, converting the prescriptive/descriptive boundary from prose to a deterministic check. Prescriptive kinds bind to directive only; descriptive kinds may never be directives; dual kinds (definition, schema) carry both moods and the choice is per belief by direction of fit.
invariants: For all active beliefs b with kind k present in this table: b.type is in row(k).allowed_types
Prescriptive kinds never appear on descriptive types; descriptive kinds never appear on directives
A kind absent from the table is unchecked, not failed - the kind enum is the closure authority

evidence.detail: The mood half of the v2 type function (type = f(mood(kind), grounding, scope)), landed with the c026-family supersessions. Bindings per design.md's three groups over the 38 c039 kinds. Preflight review per the a467/c040 precedent recorded on the plan-0 primitives this contract rests on.

new_beliefs.name: subject-containment
new_beliefs.claim: A conjunction cannot be about something its parts are not about: an active compound's subject refs must be a subset of the union of its deps' subject refs. Scope widening is the structural signature of inference - a claim whose subjects escape its deps is an inference or carries commentary to trim, never a compound. Empty subjects pass vacuously; the check earns its discriminating power only where subjects are populated.

rules.given: An active compound with non-empty subjects
rules.then: Every subject ref appears in the union of its deps' subject refs

rules.given: A compound whose subjects escape the dep union
rules.then: The node is re-typed as an inference, or its claim is trimmed to the conjunction and the widened content minted as an inference depping on it

rules.given: A compound with a dep outside the loaded belief set
rules.then: The compound is skipped, not failed - the union cannot be computed without the dep's subjects

invariants: For all active compounds c with subjects: subject_refs(c) is a subset of the union of subject_refs over deps(c)
A compound with empty subjects passes vacuously
Containment is checked on refs; subject types classify and do not partition scope

evidence.detail: The scope half of the v2 type function, with the strict-aggregate doctrine (D3, cb:a474) as its authoring-side companion. Deterministic verifier check; subject-sparse graphs triage compounds by judgment instead.

new_beliefs.name: directive-grounding
new_beliefs.claim: A prescription is adopted: an active non-contract directive grounds in deps (the beliefs it rests on) or in a stipulation artifact citing plan:, user:, session:, or document: - the record of the adoption event. Contract-grade directives may be declared standalone from policy. External-source schemes never ground a directive.

rules.given: An active non-contract directive with non-empty deps
rules.then: The directive is grounded - it rests on the beliefs it cites

rules.given: An active non-contract directive with empty deps
rules.then: The artifact must carry a stipulation scheme (plan:, user:, session:, document:) recording where the prescription was adopted
Both deps and stipulation artifact may be absent - contracts can be declared from policy

invariants: For all active non-contract directives d: deps(d) non-empty OR scheme(d.artifact) in {plan, user, session, document}
Stipulation schemes are a closed set: plan | user | session | document
External-source schemes (source, https, gmail) never ground a directive

evidence.detail: D4 confirmed, with the execution amendment admitting document: - the design's own worked re-homes (method:a1-a5, the lib: rules) are document-grounded stipulations. Legitimizes conventions as directives without manufacturing fake deps.
